impact: The daemon was selecting a task that depends on a blocked source-freshness classifier. Marking the dependent renderer blocked and appending narrow syntax-first recovery tasks prevents another broad retry loop while preserving completed work and giving the daemon useful next tasks that do not require live recrawls or authenticated automation.
summary: Defer the dependent source freshness/report-renderer work after repeated Python syntax failures and let the daemon resume independent fixture-first DevHub and missing-information tasks.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 1 task(s) are blocked: Task checkbox-54: Add a source freshness fixture that classifies stale PP&D guidance, changed content hashes, removed pages, and recrawl priority without performing a live recrawl.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
